q:
Helen was listening to music when I arrived. (Check for a/an necessity)
 Helen was listening to ___ music when I arrived.
a: Helen was listening to music when I arrived.

q:
We went to very nice restaurant last weekend. (Correct the sentence)
 We went to ___ last weekend.
a: We went to a very nice restaurant last weekend.

q:
I brush my teeth with toothpaste. (Check for a/an necessity)
 I brush my teeth with ___ toothpaste.
a: I brush my teeth with toothpaste.

q:
I use toothbrush to brush my teeth. (Correct the sentence)
 I use ___ to brush my teeth.
a: I use a toothbrush to brush my teeth.

q:
Can you tell me if there's bank near here? (Correct the sentence)
 Can you tell me if there's ___ near here?
a: Can you tell me if there's a bank near here?

q:
My brother works for insurance company. (Correct the sentence)
 My brother works for ___.
a: My brother works for an insurance company.

q:
I don't like violence. (Check for a/an necessity)
 I don't like ___ violence.
a: I don't like violence.

q:
When we were in Rome, we stayed in big hotel. (Correct the sentence)
 When we were in Rome, we stayed in ___.
a: When we were in Rome, we stayed in a big hotel.

q:
If you have problem, I'll try and help you. (Correct the sentence)
 If you have ___ , I'll try and help you.
a: If you have a problem, I'll try and help you.

q:
I like your suggestion. It's interesting idea. (Correct the sentence)
 It's ___ idea.
a: It's an interesting idea.

q:
Can you smell paint? (Check for a/an necessity)
 Can you smell ___ paint?
a: Can you smell paint?

q:
I like volleyball. It's good game. (Correct the sentence)
 It's ___ game.
a: It's a good game.

q:
Lisa doesn't usually wear jewellery. (Check for a/an necessity)
 Lisa doesn't usually wear ___ jewellery.
a: Lisa doesn't usually wear jewellery.

q:
Jane was wearing beautiful necklace. (Correct the sentence)
 Jane was wearing ___.
a: Jane was wearing a beautiful necklace.

q:
Does this city have airport? (Correct the sentence)
 Does this city have ___?
a: Does this city have an airport?

q:
key
 I couldn't get into the house. I didn't have ___.
a: I couldn't get into the house. I didn't have a key.

q:
coat
 It's very warm today. Why are you wearing ___?
a: It's very warm today. Why are you wearing a coat?

q:
ice
 Would you like ___ in your drink?
a: Would you like ice in your drink?

q:
biscuit
 Are you hungry? Have ___.
a: Are you hungry? Have a biscuit.

q:
electricity
 Our lives would be very difficult without ___.
a: Our lives would be very difficult without electricity.

q:
question
 Excuse me, can I ask you ___?
a: Excuse me, can I ask you a question?

q:
moment
 I'm not ready yet. Can you wait ___ please?
a: I'm not ready yet. Can you wait a moment please?

q:
blood
 The heart pumps ___ through the body.
a: The heart pumps blood through the body.

q:
decision
 We can't delay much longer. We have to make ___ soon.
a: We can't delay much longer. We have to make a decision soon.

q:
interview
 I had ___ for a job yesterday. It went quite well.
a: I had an interview for a job yesterday. It went quite well.

q:
day
 There are seven ___ in a week.
a: There are seven days in a week.

q:
meat
 A vegetarian is a person who doesn't eat ___.
a: A vegetarian is a person who doesn't eat meat.

q:
queue / people
 Outside the cinema there was ___ of people waiting to see the film.
a: Outside the cinema there was a queue of people waiting to see the film.

q:
joke
 I'm not good at telling ___.
a: I'm not good at telling jokes.

q:
friend
 Last night I went out with some ___ of mine.
a: Last night I went out with some friends of mine.

q:
people
 There were very few ___ in town today. The streets were almost empty.
a: There were very few people in town today. The streets were almost empty.

q:
air
 I'm going out for a walk. I need some fresh ___.
a: I'm going out for a walk. I need some fresh air.

q:
patience
 Paul always wants things quickly. He doesn't have much ___.
a: Paul always wants things quickly. He doesn't have much patience.

q:
umbrella
 I think it's going to rain. Do you have ___ I could borrow?
a: I think it's going to rain. Do you have an umbrella I could borrow?

q:
language
 How many ___ can you speak?
a: How many languages can you speak?

q:
space
 Our flat is very small. We don't have much ___.
a: Our flat is very small. We don't have much space.
